data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Instagram uses a microservices approach to divide applications into smaller, independent services.
data.technical_spec: Services are containerized and orchestrated using Kubernetes.

data.title: NoSQL Databases
data.description: The site relies heavily on NoSQL databases for data storage, accommodating large volumes of user data.
data.technical_spec: Cassandra and other NoSQL solutions ensure high availability and scalability.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: A CDN is used to improve the speed and reliability of content delivery to a global audience.
data.technical_spec: CloudFront and Akamai are potential CDN solutions.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Load balancing technology is used to distribute incoming network traffic across multiple servers.
data.technical_spec: Nginx and HAProxy could be employed for efficient traffic management.

data.title: GraphQL API
data.description: Instagram's API supports efficient data retrieval and updates through GraphQL.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes GraphQL to optimize API requests and reduce excessive calls.
